factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in the Insurance and Energy and Specialized Markets segments.

evidence.text: Revenues for our Insurance segment were $535.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to $495.3 million for the three months ended March 31, 2020, an increase of $40.3 million or 8.1%. Our underwriting & rating revenue increased $27.1 million or 7.7%. Our claims revenue increased $13.2 million or 9.0%. Revenues for our Energy and Specialized Markets segment were $156.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to $154.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2020, an increase of $2.0 million or 1.3%. The increase in Energy and Specialized Markets revenue was primarily due to increases in our environmental health and safety service revenues and core research solutions.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key segments with significant revenue increases.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, particularly in the Insurance segment.

evidence.text: Our underwriting & rating revenue increased $20.1 million or 5.7%, primarily due to an annual increase in prices derived from continued enhancements to the content of the solutions within our industry-standard insurance programs as well as selling expanded solutions to existing customers in commercial and personal lines.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raising prices in the Insurance segment indicates pricing strength.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the Insurance and Energy and Specialized Markets segments.

evidence.text: Our remaining Insurance revenue increased $31.9 million or 6.5%. The increase in Energy and Specialized Markets revenue was primarily due to increases in our environmental health and safety service revenues and core research solutions.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating in key segments, showing strong performance.

factors.summary: The Insurance and Energy and Specialized Markets segments are growing fastest, while the Financial Services segment is declining.

evidence.text: Revenues for our Insurance segment were $535.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to $495.3 million for the three months ended March 31, 2020, an increase of $40.3 million or 8.1%. Revenues for our Energy and Specialized Markets segment were $156.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to $154.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2020, an increase of $2.0 million or 1.3%. Revenues for our Financial Services segment were $34.3 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to $40.3 million for the three months ended March 31, 2020, a decrease of $6.0 million or 15.0%.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across segments with some growth and one decline.

factors.summary: Labor costs are rising, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: The remaining cost of revenues increased $5.8 million or 3.6% primarily due to increases in information technology expenses of $3.8 million, salaries and employee benefits of $3.1 million, professional consulting costs of $1.1 million, data costs of $0.4 million, and other operating costs of $0.9 million. These increases were partially offset by a decrease in travel expenses of $3.5 million. The decrease in travel expense primarily resulted from travel restrictions in connection with the COVID-19 pandemic and a reduction in travel-related costs due to the sale of our aerial imagery sourcing group in February 2020.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor costs create margin pressure, indicating a headwind.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing in the Insurance segment but improving in the Energy and Specialized Markets segment.

evidence.text: The EBITDA margin for our Insurance segment was 54.0% for the three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to 55.2% for the three months ended March 31, 2020. The EBITDA margin for our Energy and Specialized Markets segment was 34.4% for the three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to 32.0% for the three months ended March 31, 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Margins are mixed with compression in one segment and improvement in another.

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by significant share buybacks and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2021 and 2020, we repurchased $100.0 million and $173.8 million, respectively, of our common stock. For the three months ended March 31, 2021 and 2020, we also paid dividends of $47.1 million and $43.9 million, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant share buybacks and dividends indicate management confidence.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are significant, with substantial buybacks and dividend payments.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2021 and 2020, we repurchased $100.0 million and $173.8 million, respectively, of our common stock. For the three months ended March 31, 2021 and 2020, we also paid dividends of $47.1 million and $43.9 million, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Substantial buybacks and dividends show strong shareholder returns.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $448.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021 compared to $362.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2020, an increase of 23.7%. Net cash used in financing activities of $199.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021 was primarily driven by repurchases of common stock of $100.0 million, net repayments of our short-term debt of $50.0 million, and dividend payments of $47.1 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The increase in net cash provided by operating activities indicates improving free cash flow generation.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are creating value, particularly in the Insurance segment.

evidence.text: Our recent acquisitions (Franco Signor, Jornaya, and Whitespace) and dispositions (the aerial imagery sourcing group and the compliance background screening business) contributed net revenues of $8.4 million and the remaining Insurance revenue increased $31.9 million or 6.5%. Our underwriting & rating revenue increased $20.1 million or 5.7%, primarily due to an annual increase in prices derived from continued enhancements to the content of the solutions within our industry-standard insurance programs as well as selling expanded solutions to existing customers in commercial and personal lines. In addition, catastrophe modeling services and our international software solutions contributed to the growth.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisitions have significantly contributed to revenue growth in the Insurance segment, indicating successful value creation.
